answerBox.snippet: Timeouts. timeout is an integer greater than zero which specifies how many milliseconds to wait for a reply, or the atom :infinity to wait indefinitely. The default value is 5000 . If no reply is received within the specified time, the function call fails and the caller exits.
snippetHighlighted: timeout is an integer greater than zero which specifies how many milliseconds to wait for a reply, or the atom :infinity to wait indefinitely

peopleAlsoAsk.question: What is a GenServer in Elixir?
peopleAlsoAsk.snippet: Elixir GenServer is a behaviour module for implementing concurrent and stateful processes. It builds upon the concepts introduced by the gen_server module in Erlang's OTP framework, offering a more idiomatic and developer-friendly interface.
peopleAlsoAsk.title: Elixir GenServer: Concurrent Stateful Process Implementation | Curiosum

peopleAlsoAsk.question: What is a gen server?
peopleAlsoAsk.snippet: A behaviour module for implementing the server of a client-server relation. A GenServer is a process like any other Elixir process and it can be used to keep state, execute code asynchronously and so on.
